chapter_summary: The author argues that educational improvement must be guided by disinterested, mainstream research rather than the selective use of science to support ideological positions. He asserts that the decline in American education is a result of a 'lack of fit' between romantic-progressive theories and the reality of how children actually learn.

claims.text: The educational community's use of the term 'developmentally appropriate' is not sanctioned by actual scientific research.
claims.source_passage: I have already suggested that the uses made by the educational community of scientific-sounding terms like ‘developmentally appropriate’ are not really sanctioned by scientific research.

claims.text: The educational community invokes research selectively to preserve the intellectual status quo of the Thoughtworld.
claims.source_passage: the educational community invokes research very selectively to preserve the intellectual status quo of the Thoughtworld.

claims.text: Ideology and research should be disentangled to ensure research findings transcend partisanship.
claims.source_passage: Ideology and research should be disentangled as much as humanly possible. Research findings that are accurate and reliable must transcend partisanship, and must be seen to do so.

claims.text: High-quality, refereed research summarizes the most reliable accumulated educational experience available.
claims.source_passage: High-quality, refereed research summarizes the most reliable accumulated educational experience available to us.

claims.text: Good research represents the reality principle in education.
claims.source_passage: Good research represents the reality principle in education.

claims.text: Educational research in 'soft' subjects like history and psychology contains unknown factors and uncertainties.
claims.source_passage: But, since much educational research is concentrated in such ‘soft’ subjects as history, sociology, and psychology, it necessarily contains unknown factors, uncontrolled variables, and ineradicable uncertainties.

claims.text: Research is the servant of policy and can quantify trade-offs, but it cannot evaluate how to act upon them.
claims.source_passage: Research can describe and quantify the trade-offs involved in such questions, but it cannot evaluate how to act upon them. . . Research is the servant of policy, not its master.

claims.text: American educational failures have been caused by a lack of fit between dominant theories and the realities they claim to represent.
claims.source_passage: A subthesis of this book is that our failures in precollegiate education have been caused by the lack of fit between our dominant theories and the realities they have claimed to represent.

claims.text: The history of American education since the 1930s is defined by the persistence of illusion in the face of reality.
claims.source_passage: The history of American education since the 1930s has been the stubborn persistence of illusion in the face of reality.

claims.text: Modern educational reformers advocate 'discovery learning' as the primary method for achieving 'higher-order skills.'
claims.source_passage: The method advocated for achieving these ‘higher-order skills’ is ‘discovery learning,’ by which students solve problems and make decisions on their own through ‘inquiry’ and ‘independent analysis’ of ‘real-world’ projects.

claims.text: The modern method of 'discovery learning' is identical to the 'project method' advocated by Kilpatrick in the 1920s.
claims.source_passage: What Kilpatrick in the 1920s called the ‘project method’ is now called ‘discovery learning.’

claims.text: Inquiry-based learning aims to help individuals develop mental processes such as identifying and analyzing problems, stating hypotheses, and classifying data.
claims.source_passage: Inquiry is intended to do this by focusing on the development of such mental processes as identifying and analyzing problems, stating hypotheses, collecting and classifying relevant data, testing hypotheses, and coming to conclusions.

claims.text: The primary goal of inquiry-based instruction is to develop student independence and teach children how to learn for themselves using the scientific method.
claims.source_passage: It seeks to develop independence. Children are encouraged to find things out for themselves by applying the scientific method of inquiry. Through inquiry, they should learn how to learn.

claims.text: Educational reformers characterize traditional schools as 'factory-model' institutions that produce students who merely parrot facts without understanding.
claims.source_passage: This attractive picture is implicitly contrasted with traditional “factory-model” schools, which produce students who think as they are told to think and merely parrot back precooked ideas or rote-memorized facts without understanding them

claims.text: A 'strong consensus' exists among major educational organizations (like NCTM and IRA) promoting a definition of 'Best Practice' that favors student-directed and experiential learning over teacher-directed instruction.
claims.source_passage: Whether the recommendations come from the National Council of Teachers of Mathematics... the International Reading Association, the fundamental insights into teaching and learning are remarkably congruent. . . . among them are the following: LESS whole-class teacher-directed instruction . . . MORE experiential, inductive, hands-on learning.

claims.text: The 'Best Practice' consensus mandates less whole-class, teacher-directed instruction and less rote memorization of facts.
claims.source_passage: LESS whole-class teacher-directed instruction... LESS rote memorization of facts and details

claims.text: The 'Best Practice' consensus advocates for more experiential, hands-on learning and more student choice in materials like books.
claims.source_passage: MORE experiential, inductive, hands-on learning... MORE choice for students; e.g., picking their own books

claims.text: Educational reformers claim their child-centered principles are 'progressive, developmentally appropriate, and research based.'
claims.source_passage: The authors praise the current consensus on these “child-centered” principles for being “progressive, developmentally appropriate, research based, and eminently teachable.”

claims.text: The findings of mainstream research emphatically do not support the educational reforms currently recommended by the educational community.
claims.source_passage: But the findings of research emphatically do not accord with the “reforms” currently being recommended by the educational community.

claims.text: Educational reformers have neglected mainstream research in favor of nonconsensus theories, such as 'multiple intelligences,' that support progressivist goals.
claims.source_passage: In fact, many reformers have neglected mainstream research in favor of nonconsensus theories (like “multiple intelligences”) that happen to support progressivist goals and methods.

claims.text: The educational establishment uses the 'authority of science' as a conversation stopper to dismiss any alternatives to progressivist methods.
claims.source_passage: Alternatives to these “research-based” recommendations are dismissed with the scornful remark that they don’t “go along with what research is telling us about how students learn.” Such dismissals are conversation stoppers, being clothed in the authority of science.

claims.text: The National Council of Teachers of Mathematics (NCTM) rejected John Saxon's textbooks because they were considered 'too prescriptive.'
claims.source_passage: NCTM President Mary Lindquist accuses Saxon of “using techniques that have been used for years... She believes his hooks are “too prescriptive.”

claims.text: John Saxon’s math instructional approach is closer to the actual findings of mainstream research than the progressive methods advocated by the NCTM.
claims.source_passage: But in fact, Saxon’s approach is reasonably close to what research is telling us about how students learn—much closer... than are the progressive methods advocated by the NCTM.

claims.text: Mainstream publishers and the math establishment have largely refused to conduct independent evaluations of the Saxon math program.
claims.source_passage: While no independent evaluations of his program have been reported in the research literature (apparently nobody wants to undertake them)...

claims.text: John Saxon's commercial success and teacher approval indicate that American pragmatism can resist progressivist educational orthodoxy.
claims.source_passage: Saxon is a true American type—and a hopeful symbol that, in time, American pragmatism and independent thinking can make inroads against progressivist orthodoxy.

claims.text: The success of Saxon Math is evidenced by its market growth and adoption by hundreds of schools despite opposition from the mathematical establishment.
claims.source_passage: Saxon’s mainly commonsense techniques have been working well enough to persuade hundreds of schools to buck the math establishment and buy his books. He is now a multimillionaire, with book sales and market share increasing every year.

claims.text: Most teachers using Saxon Math report that students perform significantly better at math than previous cohorts.
claims.source_passage: most teachers who use his books say that their children are far better at math than their predecessors were.

claims.text: The educational research community has avoided conducting independent evaluations of John Saxon’s math program.
claims.source_passage: While no independent evaluations of his program have been reported in the research literature (apparently nobody wants to undertake them)

claims.text: The claim that American math failure is caused by traditional practices like rote memorization is highly questionable.
claims.source_passage: One of the complaints parents make is that their children are not mastering such facts. Is it possible that the ideas recommended by the NCTM are the very ideas that already pervade the schools they are supposed to transform?

claims.text: Modern reform techniques like discovery learning and thematic learning are essentially old, project-oriented, child-centered methods that have dominated American schools for decades.
claims.source_passage: These “new” methods include attention to individual needs and learning styles, discovery learning, and thematic learning. But these teaching techniques are essentially the project-oriented, child-centered methods that have long dominated American educational thought and have prevailed for decades in our schools.

claims.text: Current educational reformers are historically inaccurate when they claim their 'novel' techniques are based on recent research rather than early 20th-century progressivism.
claims.source_passage: The authors rightly question the claim that the “new” reform ideas are fundamentally different from the progressivist pedagogy already in place, and they challenge the idea that the “buzz words” originate in pure, disinterested science.

claims.text: Proponents of progressive education enlist and adapt convenient psychological theories to justify pre-existing child-centered, anti-factual goals.
claims.source_passage: On the contrary, the child-centered, antifact goals and methods came first. Then the most convenient current theories were enlisted and adapted to support them.

claims.text: The psychological theory of constructivism, which holds that learners actively construct knowledge, is valid in its broad outlines within mainstream psychology.
claims.source_passage: In its broadest outlines and most cautious elaborations, the theory is widely accepted in mainstream psychology.

claims.text: Human memory is rarely a perfect retrieval of a stored record; it is a reconstruction that can differ from the original experience.
claims.source_passage: F. C. Bartlett, in a seminal book on memory, showed that human remembering is rarely a perfect retrieval of something stored in our minds but, rather, a reconstruction that in some details may be quite different from the original experience.

claims.text: Meaningful learning and memory involve an active reconstruction of the 'gist' rather than a verbatim retrieval of information.
claims.source_passage: what we chiefly remember from reading or hearing speech is not the actual sequence of words but, rather, their gist. We don’t simply retrieve the words verbatim, we partly reconstruct them

claims.text: Because memory is an active construct, learning is also an active construction rather than passive reception.
claims.source_passage: Since schooling is based upon remembered learning, and since meaningful memory is not a purely passive record but an active construct, it follows that learning, too, is not passively received but actively constructed.

claims.text: Human memory primarily retains the 'gist' of speech or text rather than a verbatim sequence of words.
claims.source_passage: Starting in the 1960s, a series of experiments showed that what we chiefly remember from reading or hearing speech is not the actual sequence of words but, rather, their gist.

claims.text: Meaningful, constructed learning is educationally superior to rote, verbatim recall.
claims.source_passage: Constructed learning is therefore on the whole a good thing—better for most educational purposes than pure photographic or phonographic recall.

claims.text: Constructivism is a universal feature of all meaningful learning, regardless of how that learning is derived.
claims.source_passage: But constructivism is not only desirable, it is also universal. It characterizes all meaningful learning no matter how derived.

claims.text: The belief that constructivist learning theory mandates discovery-based teaching practices is a logical error known as the genetic fallacy.
claims.source_passage: The confusion of a destination with the means used to get there is a logical confusion that has even been given a name: “the genetic fallacy.” The leap from the general theory of constructivism to advocacy of the particular practice of discovery learning is overhasty and logically illegitimate.

claims.text: Listening to a lecture or reading a text is an active activity of constructing meaning, not a passive one.
claims.source_passage: The only way a student can understand what a teacher or anyone else is saying is through a complex, sometimes strenuous activity of constructing meaning from words. Hearing a lecture—in the event that one is understanding it—requires an active construction of meaning.

claims.text: Constructivism is an unreliable guide for choosing specific teaching practices because it applies to almost all learning activities.
claims.source_passage: Since most learning activity, including listening to a lecture, is constructivist, constructivism is an uncertain guide to teaching practice.

claims.text: The quality of learning depends more on a student's prior background knowledge than on the teacher's mode of instruction.
claims.source_passage: In fact, as I shall show later on, the amount of useful construction and learning that occur depends chiefly on the amount of relevant background knowledge the student already possesses rather than on the mode of instruction.

claims.text: Discovery learning is often inefficient because it takes more time and frequently results in students learning irrelevant or incorrect information.
claims.source_passage: It takes more time and is sometimes insecure in its results—insecure not in the durability of what is remembered but in the content of what is remembered. Students “discover” all sorts of things, some of them irrelevant to the purposes at hand and some of them wrong.

claims.text: Discovery learning is not specifically sanctioned or privileged by modern psychological theory over other forms of constructed learning.
claims.source_passage: In short, the term “constructivism” has become a kind of magical incantation used to defend discovery learning, which is no more sanctioned by psychological theory than any other form of constructed learning.

claims.text: The inference that constructivism justifies experiential, inductive, hands-on, and active learning is faulty.
claims.source_passage: This faulty inference is based on the assumption that other forms of learning involve mere “transmission” and “reception” instead of the active construction of knowledge.

claims.text: All meaningful learning, regardless of the instructional method used, entails the active construction of knowledge by the learner.
claims.source_passage: But all meaningful learnings, induced by any and all methods, entail such active construction.

claims.text: The educational claim that teaching process, attitude, and strategy is more valuable than teaching specific information is a selective use of research.
claims.source_passage: The results of that research should affect the credibility of the claim, universally expounded in our schools of education, that the teaching of process, attitude, and strategy is far more valuable than the teaching of mere information.

claims.text: The critical-thinking movement has expanded to include diverse goals like self-esteem and multiculturalism that are not necessarily connected to critical thought.
claims.source_passage: More recently, however, the critical-thinking movement has expanded beyond a narrow focus on logical coherence, and claims... to embrace almost everything that “current research” has shown to be desirable, whether or not the various desiderata have any connection with each other or with critical thought

claims.text: Experts self-consciously monitor and evaluate their mental strategies more than novices do.
claims.source_passage: Researchers have observed that one of the consistent differences between experts and novices is that experts self-consciously monitor and evaluate some of their mental strategies as they deploy them.

claims.text: Instruction in critical thinking has historically failed to prevent students from committing logical fallacies.
claims.source_passage: Yet researchers found that students from nations as varied as Israel, Germany, Austria, the Philippines, and the United States, including those who have been taught critical thinking, continue to fall into logical fallacies.

claims.text: Uncritical thinking is primarily caused by uninformed or misinformed premises rather than a lack of logical skill.
claims.source_passage: Usually, it isn’t the logical structure of people’s inferences that chiefly causes uncritical thinking but, rather, the uninformed or misinformed faultiness of their premises.

claims.text: General training in critical thinking and logic is practically ineffectual in improving logical performance.
claims.source_passage: The practical ineffectualness of general training in critical thinking has been confirmed in controlled experiments. People who have just finished a one-semester course in logic are only marginally more logical than people who have never taken logic.

claims.text: People are rarely able to apply procedural rules in appropriate ways to unfamiliar problems that belong to different domains, even when given hints and rules.
claims.source_passage: people are rarely able to apply procedural rules in appropriate ways to unfamiliar problems that belong to different domains.

claims.text: Specific procedural strategies are effective only when taught as an integral part of subject-matter instruction.
claims.source_passage: Of course, no one challenges the wisdom of including specific tips on procedural strategies as an integral part of subject-matter instruction.

claims.text: The research community is debating whether direct instruction in abstract logical principles or general self-monitoring strategies justifies the significant classroom time required to teach them.
claims.source_passage: a key subject of debate in the research community is whether direct instruction in thinking works sufficiently well to justify spending a lot of classroom time in consciously inculcating either abstract logical principles or general self-monitoring strategies.

claims.text: The teaching of general 'higher-order skills' is not a proven improvement over traditional subject-matter study.
claims.source_passage: one may question the current claim that teaching general “higher-order skills” is an improvement over subject-matter study.

claims.text: The current enthusiasm for metaskills is an updated version of the progressivist anti-subject-matter tradition that has historically caused American schools to decline.
claims.source_passage: The enthusiasm for metaskills could easily become an updated, “research-based” version of the progressivist antisubject-matter tradition that has already caused our schools to decline.

claims.text: Direct instruction in critical thinking leads to improved performance on the Cornell Critical-Thinking Test because informal logic is itself a subject matter.
claims.source_passage: In the case of critical thinking, it has been shown that direct instruction does lead to moderately improved performance on the Cornell Critical-Thinking Test... Informal logic is, after all, a subject matter in its own right.

claims.text: There is no settled evidence that instruction in critical thinking translates into improved real-world critical thinking.
claims.source_passage: The still-unsettled research debate concerns whether instruction in critical thinking translates into improved real-world critical thinking.

claims.text: Minor transfer effects of critical-thinking instruction are likely not worth the expenditure of significant extra instructional time.
claims.source_passage: the minor transfer effects of instruction in critical thinking are probably not worth the expenditure of significant extra instructional time.

claims.text: Teaching domain-specific metacognitive strategies is more effective for problem-solving than teaching broad, general problem-solving strategies.
claims.source_passage: Teaching these domain-specific metacognitive strategies has proved to be more useful to problem solving than teaching broad problem-solving strategies in the hope of encouraging spontaneous reconceptualizations of diverse problem types.

claims.text: The ability to know when to apply a specific strategy is gained through practice and experience rather than through isolated instruction.
claims.source_passage: That aspect of metacognitive knowledge is gained only after a great deal of practice and experience, and has proved not to be reliably teachable by isolated instruction.

claims.text: The direct teaching of efficient metacognitive strategies and the deliberate suppression of less efficient ones, such as counting on one’s fingers, may slow down the acquisition of expert mathematical skill.
claims.source_passage: He also found that the direct teaching of “efficient” metacognitive strategies, and the deliberate suppression of less efficient ones such as counting on one’s fingers, may actually slow down the achieving of expert mathematical skill!

claims.text: Children's untaught strategies are highly adaptive to their level of familiarity with subject matters.
claims.source_passage: Siegler has shown that children’s untaught strategies are highly adaptive to their level of familiarity with subject matters.

claims.text: Procedural knowledge is not of a higher order than the content knowledge with which it is associated.
claims.source_passage: it is not clear why this procedural knowledge is of a “higher order” (and therefore by implication better) than the content knowledge with which it is associated.

claims.text: Teaching self-monitoring strategies to slow or disadvantaged children might hinder rather than help their progress in learning.
claims.source_passage: Siegler is concerned that the teaching of self-monitoring strategies to slow or disadvantaged children might hinder rather than help their progress in learning.

claims.text: Research has not yet shown that large doses of metacognitive teaching succeed in speeding up learning.
claims.source_passage: research has not yet shown that large doses of metacognitive teaching succeed in doing so.

claims.text: An emphasis on metacognition may interfere with the orderly development of adaptive problem-solving strategies.
claims.source_passage: EOM may interfere with the orderly development of adaptive problem-solving strategies

claims.text: An emphasis on metacognition carries severe opportunity costs by usurping subject-matter instruction.
claims.source_passage: EOM may carry severe opportunity costs by usurping subject-matter instruction

claims.text: An emphasis on metacognition may overload working memory and thus impair learning.
claims.source_passage: EOM may overload working memory and thus impair rather than help learning

claims.text: Consciously learning the abstract principle behind a strategy may be harder and slower than implicitly learning the strategy itself.
claims.source_passage: consciously learning the abstract principle behind a strategy may be much harder than implicitly learning the strategy itself, so that teaching the abstract principle may slow down first graders’ progress.

claims.text: Preventing children from using backup strategies when they do not know answers leads to incorrect performance rather than expert skill.
claims.source_passage: Preventing them from using backup strategies when they do not know the answers leads to a great deal of incorrect performance. It is hard to see how this approach can aid learning.

claims.text: Content-oriented use of instructional time, such as background information and vocabulary, can be more fruitful than isolated metacognitive instruction.
claims.source_passage: the extensive metacognitive sessions might sometimes be spent more fruitfully on, for example, better understanding of the subject matter and the vocabulary that would be used in the upcoming reading

claims.text: Teaching students specific metacognitive strategies for reading comprehension has proved highly helpful.
claims.source_passage: Teaching students specific metacognitive strategies for reading comprehension has proved highly helpful to them; those students who have been trained in such methods read better than those who have not been so trained.

claims.text: For beginners, fundamental tasks like decoding, word recognition, and letter formation tax working memory to its limit.
claims.source_passage: For beginners in reading, mathematics, and writing, there is not much room in working memory for anything beyond the fundamental elements of the task. Decoding and word recognition tax beginning readers to the limit.

claims.text: Requiring students to perform metacognitive self-monitoring while struggling with fundamental tasks can degrade performance by taxing working memory.
claims.source_passage: If in addition to performing them we also ask children to monitor their own performances metacognitively, there is a likelihood that such demands will further tax working memory and even degrade performance rather than improve it.

claims.text: Explicit instruction in metacognition (EOM) may overload short-term memory and impair learning rather than help it.
claims.source_passage: EOM May Overload Short-Term Memory and Thus Impair Rather Than Help Learning

claims.text: For beginners in reading, mathematics, and writing, working memory is almost entirely consumed by the fundamental elements of the task.
claims.source_passage: For beginners in reading, mathematics, and writing, there is not much room in working memory for anything beyond the fundamental elements of the task.

claims.text: Asking children to perform metacognitive self-monitoring while they are still mastering basic tasks is likely to degrade their performance.
claims.source_passage: If in addition to performing them we also ask children to monitor their own performances metacognitively, there is a likelihood that such demands will further tax working memory and even degrade performance rather than improve it.

claims.text: The negative impact of metacognitive demands is most acute for beginning or ill-prepared students.
claims.source_passage: This problem becomes particularly acute with beginning or ill-prepared students.

claims.text: Experts in a skill can engage in self-monitoring only because they have automated the basic components of their tasks.
claims.source_passage: Enthusiasts for expert-style metacognition may overlook the critical fact that experts in any skill have so automated the basic components of their tasks that they have plenty of room in working memory to engage in self-monitoring.

claims.text: Novices who attempt to use expert-level strategies while neglecting prerequisites perform worse than if they had not used the strategies.
claims.source_passage: When novices try to think about expert-level aspects and neglect the prerequisites, they perform even worse than before.

claims.text: A primary aim of elementary education should be to help students automate basic processes in reading, writing, and math.
claims.source_passage: a chief aim in the elementary grades is to help students automate the maximum number of basic processes in reading, writing, and mathematics.

claims.text: Metacognitive self-monitoring is disproportionately burdensome for slower students compared to those who have automated basic processes.
claims.source_passage: An extra burden of self-conscious monitoring is much heavier for slower students than it is for students who have already automated many processes.

claims.text: Enabling students to take small, easy steps is the fastest way to help them achieve expertise.
claims.source_passage: Research into effective teaching shows that enabling students to take small, easy steps is the fastest way to help them become experts.

claims.text: The anti-fact and pro-process claims of American educators are based on deeply flawed premises.
claims.source_passage: The problem with these all-too-familiar sentiments is not that they fail to make sense but that their premises are deeply flawed.

claims.text: Children cannot be taught abstract generalized skills for articulating and solving problems without possessing a great deal of solid, specific knowledge.
claims.source_passage: it is misleading to suggest that, absent a great deal of solid knowledge, children can be taught abstract generalized skills for “articulating and solving problems germane to their world.”

claims.text: Teaching students to memorize pieces of data to the exclusion of skills results in wasted school time and student energy.
claims.source_passage: to the extent that students are forced to memorize pieces of data to the exclusion of the skills that can turn that data into information, those precious minutes—not to mention student energy—are wasted

claims.text: The teaching of general accessing, metacognitive, and thinking skills is a premature panacea that lacks a consistent basis in reality.
claims.source_passage: But the teaching of general accessing, metacognitive, and thinking skills has proved to be a premature panacea—a continually repeated slogan without a consistent basis in reality.

claims.text: The educational goal of inculcating general thinking skills is not soundly based in research.
claims.source_passage: The oft-repeated goal of the educational community—to inculcate general thinking skills—is not, then, soundly based in research.

claims.text: The idea that school-taught abstract skills for problem solving can be readily applied to the real world is a mirage.
claims.source_passage: The idea that school can inculcate abstract, generalized skills for thinking, “accessing,” and problem solving, and that these skills can be readily applied to the real world is, bluntly, a mirage.

claims.text: Thinking skills developed in one domain cannot be readily and reliably transferred to other domains.
claims.source_passage: So also is the hope that a thinking skill in one domain can be readily and reliably transferred to other domains.

claims.text: Broad-gauged thinking abilities (general competence) do exist in well-educated people.
claims.source_passage: Yet broad-gauged thinking abilities do exist. Most of us know well-educated people, even some not very bright ones, who have high general competence, can think critically about diverse subjects, can communicate well, can solve a diversity of problems, and are ready to tackle unfamiliar challenges.

claims.text: The goal of modern education should be to achieve general competence through an accurate understanding of real-world competency.
claims.source_passage: Rightly understood, then, the goal of general competence does define one important aim of modern education. The task is not to change that goal but to interpret it accurately so that it corresponds to the nature of real-world competency and can actually be achieved.

claims.text: Higher-order skills like critical thinking and independent problem solving are, without exception, dependent on a person being well-informed.
claims.source_passage: There is a great deal of evidence, indeed a consensus in cognitive psychology, that people who are able to think independently about unfamiliar problems and who are broad-gauged problem solvers, critical thinkers, and lifelong learners are, without exception, well-informed people.

claims.text: Many recent American graduates are deficient in higher-order skills because their schooling has been dominated by antifact theories.
claims.source_passage: There is also a lot of evidence that many students who have recently graduated from our schools are not well-informed, that they are deficient in higher-order general skills, and that their schooling has long been dominated by the antifact theories which are being advocated as “reforms” for the information age.

claims.text: Intellectual capital is the necessary real-world foundation for abstract skills like critical thinking and problem solving.
claims.source_passage: I said that intellectual capital is the tool of tools, the real-world foundation for the various abstract skills that are being recommended by teaching manuals

claims.text: The details of higher-level mental functions remain a subject of controversy among cognitive scientists and neurobiologists.
claims.source_passage: The descriptive details and even the broad outlines of higher-level mental functions are still subjects of controversy.

claims.text: Real-world competencies are highly complex, and there is no definitive scientific agreement on their most accurate description.
claims.source_passage: The nature of these real-world competencies is highly complex. It would be misleading to pretend that cognitive psychologists and neurobiologists have come to agreement about the most accurate description of them.

claims.text: Modern real-world skills are unavailable to individuals with limited communication skills and highly restricted vocabularies.
claims.source_passage: It is safe to say, for example, that none of these skills are available to a person with limited communication skills and a highly restricted vocabulary.

claims.text: The ability to 'learn to learn' is dependent on first having learned to understand what one is being taught.
claims.source_passage: One cannot “learn to learn” without having learned to understand what one is being taught.

claims.text: A lack of intellectual capital is a definitive indicator of a lack of critical-thinking skills.
claims.source_passage: Even if the possession of a broad vocabulary does not in itself guarantee the possession of critical-thinking skills, lack of intellectual capital is a sure index to the lack of such skills.

claims.text: Vocabulary level correlates with intellectual skill across all stages of early childhood and primary education.
claims.source_passage: The correlation of vocabulary level with intellectual skill applies not only to adults but also to children in preschool, elementary school, and middle school.

claims.text: A child's readiness to learn new information in a domain is sensitively dependent on their existing knowledge and relevant vocabulary in that domain.
claims.source_passage: Their readiness to learn new things in a domain is sensitively dependent on what they already know in that domain, as indicated by possession of a relevant vocabulary.

claims.text: Schooling should focus on arming children with a broad vocabulary as a logical requirement for developing problem-solving skills.
claims.source_passage: My insistence that schooling should arm children with a broad vocabulary is logically required by the goal of inculcating problem-solving skills.

claims.text: Linguistic skills serve as a prerequisite foundation for competencies in diverse domains like math, art, history, and science.
claims.source_passage: The linguistic prerequisite is a foundation for a whole range of competencies in domains such as mathematics, art, history, ethics, politics, and science.

claims.text: Children who leave first grade without being able to read at grade level have a very poor outlook for later success in school and economic life.
claims.source_passage: Marilyn Jager Adams, the author of an authoritative book on beginning reading, estimated that children who leave first grade without being able to read at grade level have a very poor outlook for later success in school and economic life.

claims.text: Language skills are cumulative and develop decisively in early childhood, making early deficits difficult to remediate.
claims.source_passage: Yet it touches on a profound truth about the cumulative nature of language skills—namely, that they build up rapidly and decisively in the early years, and that it is difficult to compensate for very early deficits.

claims.text: Linguistic skills are prerequisites for thinking and learning skills.
claims.source_passage: encies, they are prerequisites for them. A general look at how these linguistic skills develop will exemplify the stage-by-stage process by which most other thinking and learning skills are attained.

claims.text: Small incremental changes in early language learning produce enormous consequences later in a child's development.
claims.source_passage: Small incremental changes in early language learning can produce enormous consequences later on.

claims.text: When language and knowledge deficits are not compensated for early, it is nearly impossible to reach grade-level skills in later grades, despite intensive remediation.
claims.source_passage: But evidence from a variety of sources indicates that when this language and knowledge deficit is not compensated for early, it is nearly impossible to reach grade-level skills in later grades, despite intensive remediation.

claims.text: Educational funding should be shifted from later remedial programs to preschool, kindergarten, and early grade programs.
claims.source_passage: The policy implication of these findings is that, given academically effective early intervention, a lot of remedial money should gradually be shifted to preschool-kindergarten programs and to sustaining programs in the early grades

claims.text: Romantic doctrines of developmentalism currently prevent preschool and kindergarten programs from being academically effective.

claims.text: Reading ability in nondeaf children cannot exceed their listening ability.

claims.text: Oral-aural communication skills are primary and place a definite limit on a person’s reading and writing skills.
claims.source_passage: Oral-aural communication skills are primary, and they place a definite limit on a person’s reading-writing skills.

claims.text: The essence of reading is the comprehension of written language, not the technical skill of decoding.
claims.source_passage: While the process of decoding from letters to language is the foundation of reading, it isn’t the essence of reading, which is the comprehension of written language.

claims.text: If a child has well-developed oral-aural communication abilities, the only remaining barrier to reading is decoding fluency and accuracy.
claims.source_passage: But if children’s oral-aural communication abilities are well developed, the only barrier to their becoming good readers is lack of fluency and accuracy in their decoding skills.

claims.text: The acquisition of oral-aural speech is a natural, evolutionary process, while the acquisition of literacy is a nonnatural, culture-specific process.
claims.source_passage: The child’s original acquisition of oral-aural speech is an evolutionary based, natural process, universal in all human groups. But the acquisition of literacy is a nonnatural culture-specific process that is not universal.

claims.text: Mathematics learning follows a path from natural number sense to culture-specific conventions like base-ten math.
claims.source_passage: Math learning similarly starts from the natural, evolution-determined number sense, which we share with some other animals, and we build on that base to gain the culture-specific conventions of base-ten mathematics.

claims.text: After second or third grade, a feedback system between reading and oral language begins to enrich a child's learning experience.
claims.source_passage: Once a child learns to read with facility and comprehension, reading and being read to help the learning of new words and the gaining of corollary new knowledge. Thenceforth, a feedback system between oral and written speech enriches the child’s language and learning experience.

claims.text: Oral-aural skills (speaking and listening) serve as the essential foundation for reading.
claims.source_passage: If a young child’s speaking and listening skills have been impoverished by growing up in a limited linguistic environment, no effort should be spared to enhance those foundational oral-aural skills as a prerequisite for further literacy skills.

claims.text: A lack of early decoding facility creates a cumulative handicap by cutting children off from further language and learning experiences.
claims.source_passage: This means that a child with a good oral-aural start in life but without early facility in decoding skills is cut off from a range of further language and learning experiences that children with more decoding facility are gaining. That handicap will not only slow down the child’s skill in reading but also diminish the total amount of his or her language and learning experience.

claims.text: Early educational inequities can be significantly diminished through expert teaching in preschool through first grade.
claims.source_passage: With focused, expert teaching in preschool through first grade, these inequities can be greatly diminished.

claims.text: Ensuring every child reads at grade level by second grade is the single most effective reform for improving the quality and equity of American schooling.
claims.source_passage: Because of the sensitivity of academic progress to early conditions, the achievement of this single, attainable goal—every child reading at grade level by the end of first or second grade—would do more than any other single reform to improve the quality and equity of American schooling.

claims.text: Reading at grade level requires mastery of linguistic conventions, genres, and complex sentence forms beyond mere decoding.
claims.source_passage: To have grade-level skill means that the child has gained an understanding of certain conventions and expectations of language use and genre—for instance, that readings which start out saying “Once upon a time” are going to be stories. claims.text: Word meanings are symbols representing knowledge and experience, rather than formal structures like grammar.
claims.source_passage: Word meanings are not formal structures like grammar and syntax. They are symbols that represent ranges of knowledge and experience.

claims.text: Reading tests measure knowledge and schemas because they sample word comprehension.
claims.source_passage: The reading tests that currently determine whether children are reading at grade level sample not only decoding fluency and syntactic ability but also word comprehension. And since the sampled words stand for concepts and Schemas—that is, for knowledge—to read at grade level also means mastery of words that represent knowledge.

claims.text: Reading ability is not a purely formal skill and cannot be separated from factual information.
claims.source_passage: There is no accurate way to describe reading ability as a purely formal skill, or to remove from it the information-based knowledge disparaged as “factoids.”

claims.text: Vocabulary becomes an increasingly critical determinant of reading ability as children move up through the grades.
claims.source_passage: As the child moves up the grades, and as decoding and syntactic skills become mature and automatic, vocabulary becomes an ever more critical determinant of whether the child is reading at grade level.

claims.text: Sticht's Law: A person's reading skill cannot exceed their listening skill.
claims.source_passage: Reading skill cannot exceed listening skill.

claims.text: The belief that reading is a mechanical skill divorced from domain-specific knowledge is a mirage.
claims.source_passage: The notion that reading is a mechanical skill divorced from domain-specific knowledge is as great a mirage as the idea of formal “thinking” skills.

claims.text: Educational time should be shared between practicing reading mechanics and advancing oral-aural fluency.
claims.source_passage: It is at least as important to advance a child’s oral-aural fluency and comprehension as to practice the mechanics of reading. Both must be done.

claims.text: The metaphor of 'intellectual capital' is superior to the metaphor of 'tools' for understanding reading achievement.
claims.source_passage: A preoccupation with the mechanics of reading (necessary as they are) is a very good illustration of the inferiority of the tool metaphor as compared with the metaphor of intellectual capital. Decoding tools, once mastered, are a mere platform.

claims.text: It is at least as important to advance a child's oral-aural fluency and comprehension as it is to practice the mechanics of reading.
claims.source_passage: It is at least as important to advance a child’s oral-aural fluency and comprehension as to practice the mechanics of reading.

claims.text: An unthinking identification of communication skills with the mechanics of literacy, while neglecting knowledge and vocabulary, creates a grave handicap for student advancement.
claims.source_passage: An unthinking identification of communication/learning skills with the mechanics of literacy, and a consequent neglect of knowledge and vocabulary, is in the end a grave handicap to advancement in communication/learning skills.

claims.text: Comprehension skills are conferred by knowledge and a broad vocabulary.
claims.source_passage: Their upper potential is quite insignificant compared with that of comprehension skills that are conferred by knowledge and a broad vocabulary.

claims.text: The skill of comprehending or learning to learn requires a broad range of knowledge in specific domains, not just general vocabulary.
claims.source_passage: The profound meaning of the anecdote is that the skill of comprehending or learning to learn requires not just a broad range of words but also a broad range of knowledge in specific domains.

claims.text: Generalized learning skills do not exist independently of a broad liberal knowledge base.
claims.source_passage: it is astonishing to know that quite sophisticated people still hold to the belief that generalized learning skills do not require a broad liberal knowledge or, to use my preferred phrase, a rich endowment of intellectual capital.

claims.text: The aim of early education is to make decoding skills approach the oral-aural comprehension limit as rapidly as possible.
claims.source_passage: The aim in early education is not only to achieve a high level of oral-aural communication skills but to make the child’s decoding skills approach the oral-aural limit as rapidly as possible.

claims.text: Basic processes must be made unconscious and automatic to free the mind for critical thinking and problem solving.
claims.source_passage: Basic processes need to be made unconscious and automatic as early as possible in order to free the mind for critical thinking and problem solving.

claims.text: Working memory is limited by either the number of items or the absolute amount of time items can be functionally active.
claims.source_passage: it is now thought that the limitation is not so much the absolute number of items as the absolute amount of time that items can be functionally active in mind at one time.

claims.text: Real-world language skill consists of three aspects: mastery of formal elements, a content-rich knowledge base, and active deployment in problem-solving.
claims.source_passage: The development of real-world language skill results, then, in functional masteries which are extremely complex in their interactions but which can be schematized for convenience into three aspects: 1) mastery of the continually repeated formal elements of language to the point of automaticity, 2) the gaining of a content-rich knowledge base represented by particular word meanings and cultural conventions, and 3) the successful active deployment of these elements in comprehension and problem solving.

claims.text: Higher-level skills critically depend upon the automatic mastery of repeated lower-level activities.
claims.source_passage: Higher-level skills critically depend upon the automatic mastery of repeated lower-level activities.

claims.text: In mathematics, problem-solving skills involve two independent processes: schema acquisition and rule automation.
claims.source_passage: the development of problem-solving skills in algebra involves schema acquisition and rule automation. The processes underlying the acquisition of these two skills, as well as the amount of experience needed for skill development, appear to be independent.

claims.text: Mathematics consists of two distinct elements: content-oriented aspects (schemas) and grammar-like operations (rules).
claims.source_passage: mathematics also contains elements corresponding to particular vocabulary meanings, such as the basic number facts of addition, subtraction, and multiplication, as well as the special conventions of different problem types. Psychologists have called the repeated grammar-like operations of mathematics “rules,” and the more vocabulary-like, content-oriented aspects “schemas.”

claims.text: Schema acquisition and rule automation are independent processes that require different amounts of experience.
claims.source_passage: The processes underlying the acquisition of these two skills, as well as the amount of experience needed for skill development, appear to be independent.

claims.text: Rule automation reduces the cognitive load on working memory during problem-solving.
claims.source_passage: One of the benefits of rule automation is a reduction in the working-memory demands associated with using the procedure. The freeing of working-memory resources makes the processing of other features of the problem easier and less error-prone.

claims.text: Extensive practice is the only way to achieve rule automation in mathematics.
claims.source_passage: Rule automation, however, appears to occur only with extensive practice.

claims.text: Transfer of mathematical rules to different problem types is only possible after those rules have been automated.
claims.source_passage: Once the rules have been memorized and automatically executed for one set of problems, they are readily used to solve different types of algebra problems. Before this point, however, rules learned in the context of one algebra problem are not readily transferred

claims.text: Schema acquisition occurs relatively quickly and requires very little practice compared to rule automation.
claims.source_passage: Schema acquisition, on the other hand, appears to occur rather quickly, that is, with very little practice.

claims.text: The dual-structure of skill development observed in math is a general characteristic of all real-world problem-solving and critical-thinking skills.
claims.source_passage: The similarity of this description of the development of math skills with the pattern for the development of communication/learning skills suggests that we have hit upon a general structure for real-world problem-solving and critical-thinking skills.

claims.text: Expertise in any skill requires the automation of formal elements to free the conscious mind for critical thought.
claims.source_passage: First, expertness in the skill depends upon the automation, through a great deal of practice, of the repeated, formal elements of the skill, thus freeing the conscious mind for critical thought.

claims.text: Reliable higher-order thinking about any subject requires a broad, well-integrated base of relevant background knowledge.
claims.source_passage: Both sorts of study converge on the conclusion that, once basic underlying skills have been automated, the almost universal feature of reliable higher-order thinking about any subject or problem is the possession of a broad, well-integrated base of background knowledge relevant to the subject.

claims.text: The educational claim that factual knowledge is obsolete due to rapid change is based on a false analogy between technological and intellectual obsolescence.
claims.source_passage: The argument assumes that there is an analogy between technological and intellectual obsolescence. Educators in this tradition shore up that analogy with the further claim that factual knowledge has become a futility because of the ever-growing quantity of new facts.

claims.text: Learning general techniques for accessing information is an inefficient substitute for accumulating factual knowledge.
claims.source_passage: It is much more efficient for students to spend time acquiring techniques for organizing, analyzing, and accessing this perpetual Niagara of information.

claims.text: The claim that specific information is outmoded almost as soon as it has been learned dates back at least to Kilpatrick’s Foundations of Method (1925).
claims.source_passage: This claim goes back at least as far as Kilpatrick’s Foundations of Method (1925).

claims.text: The progressive argument for prioritizing skills over facts assumes a false analogy between technological obsolescence and intellectual obsolescence.
claims.source_passage: The argument assumes that there is an analogy between technological and intellectual obsolescence.

claims.text: The model of education that prioritizes all-purpose processing techniques over factual knowledge is a totally misleading model of higher-order thinking.
claims.source_passage: But the picture of higher thinking skills as consisting of all-purpose processing and accessing techniques is not just a partly inadequate metaphor—it is a totally misleading model of the way higher-order thinking actually works.

claims.text: Cross-checking internal knowledge is a far more reliable mode of thinking than applying formal techniques to looked-up data.
claims.source_passage: The procedure is clearly a very different and far more reliable mode of thinking than the error-prone method of applying formal techniques to looked-up data.

claims.text: Applying formal techniques to looked-up data is the inept and unreliable problem-solving device used by novices.
claims.source_passage: The method of applying formal techniques to looked-up data is precisely the inept and unreliable problem-solving device used by novices.

claims.text: The educational claim that students need only learn 'accessing skills' (how to look things up) is bankrupt because it can reinforce misconceptions with spurious exactitude.
claims.source_passage: The novice’s procedure illustrates not just the inappropriateness of the formalistic model but also the bankruptcy of the claim that students need only learn how to look things up—so-called “accessing skills.”

claims.text: Expert problem solving begins with redescribing problems in terms of the powerful concepts of a specific discipline.
claims.source_passage: Scientists’ problem solving starts with redescribing the problem in terms of the powerful concepts of their discipline.

claims.text: An important feature of higher-order thinking is cross-checking among inferences based on a number of richly connected concepts.
claims.source_passage: An important feature of higher-order thinking is this “cross checking among inferences,” based on a number of “richly connected” concepts.

claims.text: Human problem solving utilizes a process of triangulation among relevant guideposts in a landscape of preexisting knowledge.
claims.source_passage: In higher-order thinking, we situate a problem in mental space on analogy with the way we situate ourselves in physical space—through a process of crosschecking or triangulation among relevant guideposts in our landscape of preexisting knowledge.

claims.text: Higher-order thinking functions by situating problems in mental space through triangulation among relevant guideposts of preexisting knowledge.
claims.source_passage: In higher-order thinking, we situate a problem in mental space on analogy with the way we situate ourselves in physical space—through a process of crosschecking or triangulation among relevant guideposts in our landscape of preexisting knowledge.

claims.text: The claim that school-based information quickly grows outdated is implausible.
claims.source_passage: The example also illustrates the implausibility of the claim that school-based information quickly grows outdated.

claims.text: The latest science is generally the least reliable science because it is on the frontier and subject to dispute.
claims.source_passage: A philosopher of science, Nicholas Rescher, once observed that the latest science is in a sense the least reliable science, because, being on the frontier, it is always in dispute with other, rival theories—any of which may emerge victorious.

claims.text: The most needed knowledge for ethics, politics, history, and technology is usually basic, long-lived, and slow to change.
claims.source_passage: For most problems that require critical thought by the ordinary person regarding ethics, politics, history, and even technology, the most needed knowledge is usually rather basic, long-lived, and slow to change.

claims.text: The assertion that factual knowledge is changing too fast to make learning information useful lacks a carefully reasoned defense.
claims.source_passage: But I have never seen a carefully reasoned defense of the repeated assertion that, in the new age, factual knowledge is changing so fast as to make the learning of significant information useless.

claims.text: Higher-order thinking consists of a combination of operational facility and domain-specific knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The key trait to remember about higher-order thinking is its mixed character, consisting of operational facility and domain-specific knowledge.

claims.text: Generalized skepticism unsupported by accurate knowledge is no superior to generalized credulity.
claims.source_passage: It is hard to see why a generalized skepticism, unsupported by accurate knowledge, is superior to a generalized credulity, similarly unsupported.

claims.text: The ability to make 'good ballpark estimates' is the best hope for intelligent civic thought.
claims.source_passage: Our best hope for intelligent civic thought lies in our ability to make good ballpark estimates that are close enough to truth to make our decisions well-informed and sound.

claims.text: Neither rote memorization nor large conceptual generalizations are effective modes of education for higher-order thinking regarding modern complexities.
claims.source_passage: neither rote memorization nor large conceptual generalizations are effective modes of education for higher-order thinking about the complexities of the modern world.

claims.text: It is impossible to acquire more than a superficial understanding of a domain without some degree of quantitative sophistication (factual estimates).
claims.source_passage: On the other hand, it is difficult if not impossible to acquire more than a superficial understanding of a domain without some degree of quantitative sophistication about it.

claims.text: Accurate factual estimates are necessary for understanding many modern issues.
claims.source_passage: On the other hand, it has been shown that accurate factual estimates are necessary for understanding many issues.

claims.text: Generously selective factual instruction leads to accurate inferences not directly deducible from the literal facts taught.
claims.source_passage: Researchers have shown that such generously selective factual instruction leads to accurate inferences not directly deducible from the literal facts that were taught.

claims.text: Teaching a generous number of carefully chosen exemplary facts within a meaningful explanatory context is more effective for inducing insightful thinking than teaching only general principles or focusing on a single example in depth.
claims.source_passage: research has demonstrated that the teaching of a generous number of carefully chosen exemplary facts within a meaningful explanatory context is a better method for inducing insightful thinking than is any proposed alternative.

claims.text: The careful-sampling method of factual instruction results in improved accurate thinking even if students cannot later retrieve the specific literal details from memory.
claims.source_passage: This careful-sampling method works well even when (as usually happens) the literal details of the taught facts are not memorized by students, and cannot be retrieved accurately from memory after a period of several months.

claims.text: There is an unavoidable interdependence between relational knowledge and factual knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The conclusion from cognitive research shows that there is an unavoidable interdependence between relational and factual knowledge

claims.text: Teaching a broad range of factual knowledge is essential to effective thinking both within and among domains.
claims.source_passage: teaching a broad range of factual knowledge is essential to effective thinking both within domains and among domains.

claims.text: No state board or school district has abandoned the principle of requiring broad subject matter in elementary school despite the popularity of the progressive 'antifact motif.'
claims.source_passage: Despite the popularity of the antifact motif in our progressive education tradition... no state board or school district has yet abandoned the principle of requiring a broad range of different subject matters in elementary school.

claims.text: In the final years of high school and beyond, the educational balance of utility shifts toward deeper and more narrowly specialized training.
claims.source_passage: in the last two years of high school, and later on, the balance of utility shifts in favor of deeper and more narrowly specialized training as the best education for the modern world.

claims.text: Early educational breadth is the prerequisite for successful later specialization.
claims.source_passage: If schooling is going to become more and more specialized in later life, it is ever more important to map out the wider intellectual landscape accurately and well in the earlier years.

claims.text: Wide knowledge and a broad vocabulary supply 'entry wedges' into unfamiliar domains, enabling lifelong learning.
claims.source_passage: A wide range of knowledge and a broad vocabulary supply entry wedges into unfamiliar domains, thus truly enabling “lifelong learning,” as well as the attainment of new knowledge and greater depth as needed.

claims.text: Early schooling must map out a wide intellectual landscape accurately to prepare for later specialization.
claims.source_passage: If schooling is going to become more and more specialized in later life, it is ever more important to map out the wider intellectual landscape accurately and well in the earlier years.

claims.text: Failing to provide a broad early education produces narrow, ignorant thinkers who are subject to delusion and rhetoric.
claims.source_passage: Otherwise, we shall produce not critical thinkers but narrow, ignorant ones, subject to delusion and rhetoric.

claims.text: Modern education should increase the challenge and breadth of early education rather than deferring extensive knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The unmistakable implication for modern education is that, instead of constantly deferring the introduction of challenging and extensive knowledge, we need to be taking the opposite tack by increasing both the challenge and the breadth of early education.

claims.text: Vague curriculum guidelines guarantee significant gaps and repetitions in student knowledge.
claims.source_passage: I described in some detail how the neglect of intellectual capital in favor of intellectual tools has produced curriculum guidelines of such vagueness that they guarantee significant gaps and repetitions in the knowledge that our students receive

claims.text: Gaps and repetitions in curriculum have extremely serious adverse consequences for higher-order thinking.
claims.source_passage: The adverse consequences of this curriculum defect on higher-order thinking are serious in the extreme.

claims.text: Higher-order thinking requires both breadth of factual knowledge and specific points of depth.
claims.source_passage: Cognitive science has shown that higher-order thinking requires both breadth of factual knowledge and points of depth as well.

claims.text: The best tool for higher-order thinking is intellectual capital, defined as knowing facts plus domain-appropriate procedures.
claims.source_passage: The best tool for higher-order thinking is intellectual capital—that is, to know a lot, not just facts but also the domain-appropriate procedures and strategies for dealing with them.

claims.text: Scientific confidence is built when independent sources of research converge on the same result.

claims.text: A consensus regarding effective teaching methods has emerged from three independent research sources.

claims.text: Small-scale pairings, basic cognitive research, and large-scale international studies yield highly consistent findings on pedagogy.
claims.source_passage: The findings from all three sources are highly consistent with each other regarding the most effective pedagogical principles.

claims.text: Cognitive psychology is currently the most reliable source of insight into the processes of learning.
claims.source_passage: cognitive psychology—which is currently our best and most reliable source of insight into the processes of learning.

claims.text: Findings from classroom observations, cognitive psychology, and international comparisons are highly consistent regarding effective pedagogical principles.
claims.source_passage: The findings from all three sources are highly consistent with each other regarding the most effective pedagogical principles.

claims.text: Time spent focused on content and the total amount of content taught are more significant factors in student learning than specific teacher behaviors.
claims.source_passage: time spent focused on content and the amount of content taught were more important factors than the teacher behaviors that were used to teach the content.

claims.text: Instructional methods should be tailored to student age; for example, younger students learn better through question-and-answer modes while seventh graders may benefit equally from lectures.
claims.source_passage: With seventh graders, it did not matter whether the teacher used questions and student responses or gave straight lectures. But younger students, for example, third graders, learned better with the question-and-answer mode.

claims.text: Academic achievement in reading and math is significantly higher in programs with a strong academic focus compared to those using a project-method approach.
claims.source_passage: Programs having strong academic focus rather than programs using the project-method approach produced the highest gains in reading and math.

claims.text: Direct factual questions and the classical recitation pattern correlate positively with student achievement.
claims.source_passage: Almost anything connected with the classical recitation pattern of teacher questioning (particularly direct factual questions rather than more open questions) followed by student response, followed by teacher feedback, correlated positively with achievement.

claims.text: A 'heavily affective' teaching style focused on self-esteem and psychic well-being produces lower academic achievement than a businesslike, academic focus.
claims.source_passage: Teachers who produced the least achievement used a “heavily affective” approach and were more concerned with the child’s self-esteem and psychic well-being than with academics.

claims.text: Learner-centered discovery learning is ineffective compared to academic-centered modes of instruction.
claims.source_passage: The biggest contrast was not between modes of academic instruction but between all such instruction and “learner-centered” “discovery learning,” which was ineffective.

claims.text: Students in structured, whole-class environments are more engaged and motivated than those in student-centered classrooms.
claims.source_passage: Students are more engaged and motivated in these classrooms than in student-centered ones.

claims.text: Effective reading instruction requires that decoding skills be mastered to the point of automaticity through overlearning.
claims.source_passage: Decoding skills mastered to the point of overlearning (automaticity).

claims.text: Whole-class instruction is more effective for achievement than the fragmented or individualized methods often favored in progressive pedagogy.
claims.source_passage: They used whole-class instruction most of the time.

claims.text: Learning is most efficient when new material is challenging but can be easily assimilated into a student's existing knowledge base.
claims.source_passage: learning proceeded best when the material was somewhat new and challenging, but could also be assimilated relatively easily into what students already knew.

claims.text: Academic learning is primarily influenced by the amount of time students spend engaged in appropriate academic tasks.
claims.source_passage: One is that academic learning is influenced by the amount of time students spend in appropriate academic tasks.

claims.text: Students learn more efficiently when teachers structure new information and help students relate it to prior knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The second is that students learn more efficiently when their teachers first structure new information for them and help them relate it to what they already know

claims.text: Higher-level learning objectives cannot be achieved with relative ease through discovery learning by the student.
claims.source_passage: Such success will not be achieved with relative ease through discovery learning by the student.

claims.text: Drill and practice are essential to complex and creative intellectual performance.

claims.text: Instruction centered on content is motivationally superior to learner-centered instruction for young learners.

claims.text: Individualized, learner-centered instruction is boring to most students because they are not receiving individualized attention most of the time.
claims.source_passage: individualized, learner-centered instruction must be extremely boring to most students most of the time, since, by mathematical necessity, they are not receiving individualized attention most of the time.

claims.text: The inherent satisfaction in skill mastery and interest in subject matter is the best way to engage young children.

claims.text: New learnings should only be introduced after students have mastered old learnings, though not necessarily to the point of overlearning for non-procedural content.
claims.source_passage: New learnings should not be introduced until feedback from students indicates that they have mastered the old learnings quite well, though not, as in the case of procedural skills, to the point of overlearning.

claims.text: Students who have mastered word recognition through structured practice are significantly better able to comprehend what they are reading.
claims.source_passage: Students who have mastered word recognition through structured practice of that procedural task, for example, are much better able to comprehend what they are reading.

claims.text: The repeated-reading method produces a transfer-of-training effect, making the initial reading of new, unfamiliar passages faster.
claims.source_passage: students not only improved in fluency on each passage, they also showed a transfer-of-training effect in that the first reading of each new passage was faster than the previous initial reading had been

claims.text: Effective teaching requires monitoring student performance and providing corrective feedback during drill or practice.
claims.source_passage: and then monitor their performance and provide corrective feedback during recitation, drill, practice, or application activities.

claims.text: Repeated readings is one of the most effective methods for overlearning word recognition.

claims.text: Automating word recognition is a prerequisite for comprehension because it frees the mind's working memory.

claims.text: New content must be taught in small incremental steps due to the inherent limitations of working memory.

claims.text: Distributed practice is superior to massed practice for ensuring retention in long-term memory.

claims.text: Content learning has not occurred until the content is fixed in long-term memory through content rehearsal.
claims.source_passage: Whatever practical arrangements are chosen for classroom learning, the principle of content rehearsal is absolutely essential for fixing content in long-term memory. Until that fixation occurs, content learning cannot be said to have happened.

claims.text: Continual feedback from students is essential to effective teaching.
claims.source_passage: That receiving continual feedback from the students is essential to good teaching is a robust finding in all the studies

claims.text: Verbal comprehension is heavily dependent on a student's relevant background knowledge.

claims.text: Providing summaries or 'holistic clues' at the start of a lesson improves comprehension by giving students a framework for assimilation.

claims.source_passage: When young children are expected to learn on their own, particularly in the early stages, the students run the danger of not attending to the right cues, or not processing important points, and of proceeding on to later points before they have done sufficient elaboration and practice.

claims.text: Research in cognitive psychology regarding working memory and long-term memory explains the effectiveness of structured classroom practices.
claims.source_passage: Findings concerning working memory, long-term memory, and the best prior conditions for meaningful learning explain the effectiveness of almost all the practices that were found to be effective in the classroom studies.

claims.text: Young students achieve more when receiving instruction from a teacher rather than learning on their own or from peers.
claims.source_passage: It also explains why young students who receive their instruction from a teacher usually achieve more than those who are expected to learn new materials and skills on their own or from each other.

claims.text: Self-directed learning in young children carries the risk of students failing to attend to correct cues or process important points.
claims.source_passage: When young children are expected to learn on their own, particularly in the early stages, the students run the danger of not attending to the right cues, or not processing important points

claims.text: Asian countries consistently rank at the top of international math achievement while the United States ranks at the bottom.
claims.source_passage: In international comparative studies of math achievement among developed nations, Asian countries rank at the top, the United States at the bottom.

claims.text: Classroom practice is a highly important factor in determining the achievement gap between Asian and American students.
claims.source_passage: Nonetheless, classroom practice is a highly important factor in determining these results.

claims.text: The achievement gap between American and Asian students is not caused by the greater 'diversity' of the American student population.
claims.source_passage: Stevenson and Stigler effectively dispose of the argument that our inferior classroom results are owing to our greater “diversity.”

claims.text: Superior pedagogy is atypical in the United States compared to international peers.
claims.source_passage: Unfortunately, as comparative studies show, such superior pedagogy is not at all typical in the United States.

claims.text: Effective classrooms maintain a social atmosphere that is warm but businesslike and focused on work.
claims.source_passage: In the best classrooms, the social atmosphere was warm and supportive, but at the same time businesslike and focused on the job at hand.

claims.text: Classrooms focused on verbal praise and 'self-esteem talk' are among the worst-performing.
claims.source_passage: By contrast, the worst-performing classrooms were “heavily affective,” with a lot of verbal praise and self-esteem talk.

claims.text: Excessive teacher praise discourages student improvement and cuts off discussion.
claims.source_passage: Praise cuts off discussion and highlights the teacher’s role as the authority. It also encourages students to be satisfied with their performance rather than informing them about where they need improvement.

claims.text: Discussing student errors is an effective technique for clarifying misunderstandings and involving students in assessing alternative solutions.
claims.source_passage: Discussing errors helps to clarify misunderstandings, encourage argument and justification, and involve students in the exciting quest of assessing the strengths and weaknesses of the various alternative solutions that have been proposed.

claims.text: Effective teaching begins with a brief statement of goals for the day's class and a connection to previous knowledge.
claims.source_passage: The best teachers introduce new material in small, easily mastered steps, setting a deliberate but brisk pace, not moving ahead until students show that they understand. Better results come from teachers who move forward with new concepts, have higher expectations, and provide review, but not “incessant review.”

claims.text: In high-performing Asian classrooms, teachers and students work together as a group toward goals described at the start of the period.
claims.source_passage: the Japanese elementary school class—like those in Taiwan and China—consists of teacher and students working together toward the goals described at the beginning of the class.

claims.text: American mathematics classrooms are frequently fragmented by non-academic interruptions and individual work monitoring.
